primary_sources.title: Federation of Malaya Independence Act 1957 (UK); Federation of Malaya Agreement 1957
Federal Constitution of the Federation of Malaya (1957), drafted on Reid Commission report
Report of the Federation of Malaya Constitutional Commission (Reid Commission), 1957

statutory_basis: Federation of Malaya Independence Act 1957 (UK), Federal Constitution of the Federation of Malaya 1957
institutional_scope: Federation of Malaya (Peninsular Malaysia plus Penang and Melaka) — independence from British colonial rule. Constitutional monarchy with YDPA elected from nine hereditary Rulers. Article 153 (special position of Malays); Article 3 (Islam as religion of the Federation); Article 11 (freedom of religion); Article 8 (equality). The consociational compromise codified.
notes: The founding pact. Independence achieved by Alliance Party (UMNO-MCA-MIC) negotiation under Tunku Abdul Rahman leadership. The Reid Commission's report (which used 'a bargain not a contract' framing) underlies the constitutional architecture but the phrase 'social contract' is not in the constitution itself (see T4A issue 1626). This event creates the institutional substrate on which P3 onwards unfolds.
